backdrops.title: Act One
backdrops.note: Establish the world and the problem

attributes.function: Establish the world, introduce the protagonist and the disruption
attributes.dramatic_question: Who is responsible for the disappearances?
attributes.shift: Ezra accepts he cannot ignore what is happening

attributes.function: Escalation — the threat grows, alliances form and fracture
attributes.dramatic_question: Can Ezra trust anyone in Harrow Creek?
attributes.shift: The truth about the Tillamook land is exposed

cards.title: Ezra Cade
cards.noteRaw: Confederate veteran, former Pinkerton agent. Haunted by what he did during the war and what he enabled after it.

cards.title: Harrow Creek
cards.noteRaw: A dying timber town in 1870s Oregon. The surrounding forest belongs — by treaty — to the Tillamook people.

cards.title: The Body in the Mill
cards.noteRaw: Ezra arrives in Harrow Creek. A body has been found — partially consumed. The sheriff wants it buried quietly.

cards.title: Complicity
cards.noteRaw: The story asks what we owe to the harms we enabled, even at a distance.

entities.title: Ezra Cade
entities.noteRaw: Confederate veteran, former Pinkerton agent. Haunted by what he did during the war and what he enabled after it.

attributes.summary: A man who enforced injustice for pay and is not sure he deserves redemption — but keeps trying anyway.

entities.title: Harrow Creek
entities.noteRaw: A dying timber town in 1870s Oregon. The surrounding forest belongs — by treaty — to the Tillamook people.

attributes.description: Muddy main street, a half-built church, a mill that barely runs. Everyone is watching everyone else.

entities.title: The Body in the Mill
entities.noteRaw: Ezra arrives in Harrow Creek. A body has been found — partially consumed. The sheriff wants it buried quietly.

attributes.goal: Establish Ezra's arrival and the inciting disruption
attributes.conflict: The sheriff actively obstructs any investigation
attributes.outcome: Ezra decides to stay — he has seen this kind of cover-up before

entities.title: Complicity
entities.noteRaw: The story asks what we owe to the harms we enabled, even at a distance.

attributes.statement: Silence in the face of injustice is its own form of violence
attributes.expression: Ezra's reluctant investigation mirrors his own reckoning
attributes.opposition: Some harms cannot be undone — only witnessed
